reviews.review_description:
Having been a very happy user of Google Earth VR for a while now I have only recently become aware of standalone apps using Google imagery that don't require a fairly high end PC to operate. I first tried Earthquest, which is slightly awkward to get running and needs a Google account to obtain something called an API key. This app is my most recent acquisition in the same field. The main advantage of this one is that it works straight away, and that is a huge difference for anyone who isn't particularly tech savvy. Also the controls for flying around are better on this one. However, I have found the image quality on Earthquest to be significantly better as it has settings adjustment for these factors. Earthquest also has Google Street View integration, which this one doesn't have (at time of writing). Both apps are priced at £3.99, which is very reasonable considering I paid £1000 for the laptop I use for running Google Earth VR. The full Google offering is still very much King of this domain however, with far superior graphics quality, but both this app and Earthquest are very much worthwhile alternatives for constrained budgets.
UPDATE 
This app seems to have stopped working. No idea what's happened to it. It seemed fine last time I tried it, but now, 9th September 2024, none of the Google Earth imagery opens and you just get a strange looking blank screen. I would suggest you don't buy this until it is confirmed to be working again.

developer_response.body: we just uploaded an update that should fix your problem!

reviews.review_description:
App is not working, you can only browse menu and nothing else. I do not recommend.
Edit: there is no new update. Last version us 5.2. Which version is fixed?
Edit 20.8.2024: next 12 days and still nothing? I find it very strange...

developer_response.body: we just uploaded an update that should fix your problem!

developer_response.body:
hi, we are sorry to hear that. can you tell us if the problem happens when you free search or when you choose from a predetermined place?
btw another person said this to us and we just uploaded a version to fix this 5.12, please make sure you are on the correct one.
Thanks!

developer_response.body:
We just uploaded an update fixing this!
You can try now!

reviews.review_title: We want to use It not.only in english
reviews.review_description: Hello! I'd like to use It without the need to change the system language to english and to have to restart my quest 3 every time! It's a amazing app except for this.Please repair It! Thanks.

instant headacbe at the loading screen which is fixed..
too fast movement with joystick
bad details, no enjoy at all
will refund  :(

This is the first real standalone Google Earth experience in Oculus store, while there's already a similar APP in SideQuest called EarthQuest. So I'll compare these two.
Flying is a lot smoother in EarthQuest.
The graphics are at about the same level - you can't expect too much on a standalone headset.
I like the blue sky background in AnyWR over the MR background in EarthQuest.
AnyWR has search function while EarQuest doesn't.
Like in some other games with really large maps, there seems to be a limit on how far you can see in anyWR. So when you fly high in the sky, you can't see the horizon beyond that limit. All you can see is the "event horizon" of that limit, which is not ideal. While somehow EarthQuest dosen't have this limit. In Earthquest you can see as far as the edge of the Earth.
You pass through buildings and even go under ground in anyWR, which is weird.
I think overall EarthQuest offers a better experience mainly because flying is smoother, no sight limit issue and doesn't go under ground.  That leaves room for improvements for this APP, and I hope this APP gets better with future updates.

App is blinking in one eye on Quest 3. I will change my review when its fixed.

Edit: sure, heres how it looks https://youtu.be/2TpqkiK3s_4 . Probably single pass problem.

developer_response.body: we just uploaded an update 4.7 and it should fix your problem

reviews.review_description:
It's early days for this app but I think it has potential. Really enjoyed visiting a few places. Granted, at street level the graphics leave something to be desired but stay 'up and above' and it looks very good indeed. I like the Wander app but this offers something different and as I don't have a PC powerful enough for PCVR I'm interested to see how this develops. 
I'd like to request that the speed adjustment is remembered so that you don't have to set it every time. 
And it's priced at less than the cost of a pint! Well done Devs, keep at it.
Thank you

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback and we're glad you liked it!
We'll update your comment about the settings in the next version update, great point!
Just so you know, we just updated a new version with over 1500 locations to choose from in specific cities.

reviews.review_description:
The app is stuck at the map loading screen, then nothing happens.
Update: no i have no internet connection issue, everything is working exept this app which does not load any map, i'm stuck with this pink bubble floating above some blue low res ground, i've waited more than ten minutes several times and nothing happens...

update 2: even after the update, still nothing happens, see my video, my connection is working fine https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ThBAjUq8TjY

developer_response.body: we figured out how to fix the problem for now, you need to change the language on your quest from what is it to English does not matter if to UK or US. and we will make it work without changing the language as soon as we can!

reviews.review_description: Just downloaded today. App starts, goes through some instructions, allows menu selection for destinations, arrives to blank screen.

developer_response.body:
just uploaded an update the fixes the issue
thanks!

reviews.review_description:
I really like this app idea. Flying smoothly around the world is super fun. However, the resolution is very poor, especially when you get closer to ground level. 
The price is reasonable ($5), but it needs a lot of development, particularly in significantly improving the resolution. 
I won't ask for a refund and will keep it, hoping it motivates the developer to increase the resolution.

reviews.review_title: Closest thing to google earth on quest
reviews.review_description:
So the app is p rough at the moment but a few improvements and some optimizations it could be amazing. Few issues are snap turning to the left doesn’t work properly. Also flying up and down with controllers instead of being locked to a flat plane would really help the experience. In terms of visuals I think they could leverage the SSW quest screen warping and some foviated rendering to get better graphics. Hope to see some improvements but really cool someone is making this available to quest users

Edit: appreciate the devs commenting. I’m aware of the up and down grip controls I meant it would be nice to fly where you’re pointing instead of being locked to manually moving on the z axis by using the grips to go up and down

developer_response.body:
hi,
1. We will fix the left turn bug in the next version update.
2. You can fly up and down, by pressing the trigger and grip button on the left hand, as shown on the first screen when you open the app.

developer_response.body: we told you, to fix the problem you need to set the language on you quest system to English doesn't matter if us or UK

reviews.review_description:
I'm excited for this. I have some suggestions. The search UI should be able to be seen the surroundings. There should be a globe so we can see where we are. Also, in the future I hope for street view. 
Edit:
The loading screen being anchored to my head makes me dizzy, and I never even had to get VR legs. Also, the colour scheme is torturous to look at. Maybe blue and white would be better, to reflect the main colour of the Earth.

reviews.review_title: Great to finally access Google Earth VR
reviews.review_description:
Video test here (remove the space before 'be');

https://youtu. be/8gURa9s3nok?si=YrxDmc7PU8lW5_ei

It's wonderful that Google Earth can be experienced in standalone VR! Seeing everything in 3D and flying around is so cool!

I just wish the textures would load in a bit faster, perhaps make it so you can select an area to load fully manually, rather than it relying on where you're looking? 

Great work, have really been waiting for this!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review!
We uploaded a new version with many bugs fixed, and the ability to save your settings as well.
Regarding the textures and loading, most of it is streamed by Google, but we'll look into improving this.

reviews.review_title: Travel the world from your armchair
reviews.review_description:
After a few initial problems with the app, which were quickly and effectively fixed by the developer, I have spent a good amount of time flying the world around watching mountains and towns. Of course the resolution is not super fine, as it is a small processor in the headset,handling a lot of data, but it is quite OK.

On my wishlist for further development is the ability to see on a map where I am, once I have moved some way from the starting point, maybe some overlay with names, that can be turned on and off like in Google earth?

I absolutely recommend trying out this app, it's very cheap, and I look forward to see further development

My PC can't handle Google Earth so this is a fun alternative. However, some locations take a very long time to fully load or just don't fully load at all. The app occasionally crashes. Buttons occasionally become non-responsive. It is worth $5 but leaves you wishing was just a little bit more polished.

developer_response.body:
Hi, we just uploaded an update that should make the game run smoother, please check!
Thank you

reviews.review_title: Need higher resolution and non 3D mode
reviews.review_description: My first experience with Google Earth VR without PC. If possible, Please update to improve the resolution on the Quest 3. It is a smooth enough experience. The low resolution is the problem. Is it possible to include a mode without 3D building? Just the satellite map and terrain? That would be useful too. I Support this app.
